question.label: Expectation gap
question.name: Where do my expectations for your work feel different from yours right now?
question.description: Names alignment gaps without assuming the answer, and makes the mismatch discussable.

answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.

assessment.note: He names a mismatch in emphasis — wanting faster growth in concept rationale over execution speed — which is a strong expectations-clarity gap and mild positive engagement signal; also reveals a growth thread worth following.

new_queue.label: Concept rationale
new_queue.name: Concept rationale — what does stronger work there look like to you?

new_queue.label: Growth blocker
new_queue.name: What is getting in the way of the next skill you want to build?

new_queue.label: Two-week focus
new_queue.name: What's the first concrete change you want to make before we meet again?

history.answer_excerpt: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
